ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for chicken yakitori donburi with daikon and ponzu. FOODON_00002572 (chicken dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Chicken meat dishes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for yakitori donburi with daikon and ponzu in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to chicken yakitori donburi. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular Japanese dish codes at this level.

serving.description: 1 bowl (combo with miso soup or drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of steamed rice topped with grilled chicken yakitori, diced and grated daikon, ponzu sauce, and a half-boiled egg, served with miso soup or a drink.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Yakitori (grilled chicken skewers) and Japanese rice bowl composite — estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries: Chicken, broilers or fryers, thigh, meat only, cooked, roasted (FDC ID 171477); Rice, white, short-grain, cooked (FDC ID 169760); Daikon radish, raw (FDC ID 169231); Ponzu sauce (FDC ID 2003573); Egg, whole, cooked, hard-boiled (FDC ID 173424)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for this composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging: approximately 35% cooked rice (~158 g), 30% grilled chicken thigh (~135 g), 15% daikon radish (~68 g), 10% ponzu sauce (~45 g), 8% half-boiled egg (~36 g), 2% miso soup solids (~9 g). Per-100g values derived from this 450 g composite estimate. Values are moderate-confidence estimates.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — White rice (Japanese short-grain, cooked) GI ~54–56; composite bowl GI estimated by weighted carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. The dominant glycemic driver is Japanese short-grain white rice (GI ~54, University of Sydney GI Database). Grilled chicken, daikon, egg, and ponzu sauce contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The composite GI is estimated at 54 (low), anchored to the rice component. Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (54 × 59.4 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 32.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from ponzu sauce and miso soup
hypertension.guidance: Each serving provides an estimated 1,395 mg sodium (310 mg per 100 g × 450 g), representing approximately 60–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced ponzu and miso or choose a lower-sodium variant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index driven by white rice component, but with moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The bowl has a low GI of approximately 54 and a glycemic load of ~32.1 per serving, primarily from white rice; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller rice portion, substitute with brown rice if available, and pair with the protein and daikon components to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean grilled chicken with low saturated fat; moderate sodium concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled chicken thigh provides lean protein with relatively low saturated fat (~0.8 g per 100 g), and daikon contributes antioxidants and fibre; however, the sodium load from ponzu and miso warrants attention for individuals with cardiovascular disease who are sodium-sensitive.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Yakitori Bowl with Diced and Grated Daikon and Ponzu Sauce is a Japanese donburi (rice bowl) dish featuring grilled chicken skewers (yakitori) served over steamed Japanese short-grain white rice, topped with a combination of finely diced and freshly grated daikon radish, and finished with a drizzle of ponzu sauce — a light, citrus-soy condiment made from soy sauce, yuzu or sudachi juice, mirin, and dashi. The dish is typically served as a combo with a half-boiled egg and miso soup or a beverage. The grilled chicken provides a smoky, savoury flavour with a slightly charred exterior, while the daikon adds a crisp, refreshing contrast and mild peppery bite. Ponzu sauce contributes a bright, tangy, umami-rich finish that balances the richness of the chicken and egg. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-calorie, protein-rich meal (~118 kcal per 100 g, ~9.2 g protein per 100 g) with moderate carbohydrate content from the rice. Sodium is elevated due to ponzu and miso, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is low (~54), driven by white rice, and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 32.1, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The daikon component provides vitamin C and dietary fibre, supporting digestive health. This dish is commonly found at Japanese casual dining chains in Singapore such as Sukiya.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging of USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked chicken thigh, Japanese short-grain rice, daikon radish, ponzu sauce, and boiled egg. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for Japanese white rice as the dominant glycemic driver. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and restaurant-level variability in portion sizes and sauce quantities.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component USDA source entries used for this composite estimate. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients at this level of detail. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as selenium values are not reported in all component USDA entries used. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in grilled chicken and plant-based components per USDA FoodData Central. 5. The dish is sold as a combo at Sukiya (Funan) and Funan Mix & Match in Singapore at SGD 11.20. 6. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Japanese donburi combo portions; actual restaurant portion may vary. 7. Ponzu sauce contains soy, fish-derived dashi, and gluten; the dish is not suitable for gluten-free, halal-certified, or vegan diets without modification. 8. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate anchored to Japanese short-grain white rice per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with composite nutrient estimation from USDA FoodData Central components.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for q4_glycemic_completeness. Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 54 falls within the low GI range (<=55). Recalculated `glycemic_load_per_serving` to 32.1 based on available carbohydrate (13.8 - 0.6 = 13.2g/100g * 4.5 = 59.4g for 450g serving) and updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance` accordingly. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array; confirmed all Japanese-script and romanized entries present verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed GI is numeric >0. Confirmed no zero core macros without justification. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
